title: Holden rejects second MBTA Communities proposal, faces legal action
author: Annie Jonas
bullets: Holden rejected a proposal to comply with the MBTA Communities Act with a vote of 520 to 257.
The proposal aimed to create multifamily overlay districts for 759 housing units, but faced skepticism over development feasibility.
Holden now faces legal action from the Attorney General's Office for noncompliance with the MBTA Communities Act.

title: Civil rights group wants AG to address LGBTQ-related bullying in school hockey
author: Abby Patkin
bullets: Lawyers for Civil Rights urges AG Andrea Campbell to tackle rise in LGBTQ-related bullying in school hockey.
Complaints include homophobic slurs, intimidation, and exclusion of gay students from school hockey programs.
The group demands Massachusetts Interscholastic Athletic Association take action against identity-based harassment.

title: Al Horford supports Luke Kornet’s stance on Hawks’ ‘Magic City’ promotion
author: Khari A. Thompson
bullets: Luke Kornet criticized the Atlanta Hawks' 'Magic City' promotion, questioning its alignment with the NBA's values.
Al Horford endorsed Luke Kornet's stance on X, sharing his former Celtics teammate's letter against the promotion.
The promotion features a performance by T.I. and Magic City wings, scheduled for March 16 against the Orlando Magic.

title: Patriots wide receiver Stefon Diggs scrubs Instagram account
author: Khari A. Thompson
bullets: Stefon Diggs deleted all posts from his Instagram account, maintaining only temporary Instagram Story content.
Diggs faces a pretrial hearing on April 1 for assault and strangulation charges involving a personal chef.
The Patriots must decide by March 13 whether to guarantee Diggs's $6 million signing bonus for the 2026 season.

title: Get one-way flights for as low as $79 during JetBlue’s spring sale
author: Kristi Palma
bullets: JetBlue's spring sale offers one-way flights from Logan Airport starting at $79, valid for travel April 14 to June 10.
Discounted destinations include Daytona Beach for $79, Los Angeles for $119, and London for $229, with some blackout dates.
The sale ends March 5 at 11:59 p.m. EST and applies to Tuesday and Wednesday travel; tickets available on jetblue.com.

title: All the new movies and TV shows streaming in March
author: Kevin Slane
bullets: Hulu adds Oscar-nominated films 'The Secret Agent' and 'It Was Just an Accident' starting March 1, 2026.
Netflix debuts the second season of 'One Piece' on March 10 and 'Peaky Blinders: The Immortal Man' on March 20.
HBO Max features new series 'Rooster' with Steve Carell on March 8, and 'The Comeback' on March 22.

title: Husband and wife charged with throwing rocks at passing cars in Southwick
author: Abby Patkin
bullets: Celestine Robinson and David Ducharme from Woonsocket charged with throwing rocks at cars in Southwick, Massachusetts.
Robinson faces additional charges including drunk driving with a blood alcohol level of 0.127, above the legal limit.
Ducharme held without bail due to past legal issues; Robinson's bail set at $1,500 for similar reasons.

title: With deadline looming, still no word on security funding for World Cup in Foxborough
author: Molly Farrar
bullets: Foxborough officials seek $7.8 million for World Cup security at Gillette Stadium, deadline for funding decision is March 17.
Boston 2026 committee claims contractual obligation for funding, but actual responsibility remains unclear between them and FIFA.
Massachusetts awaits over $46 million federal security funding; decision delayed by Department of Homeland Security and FEMA.

title: Lamplighter Brewing Co. workers vote to join union
author: Katelyn Umholtz
bullets: Lamplighter Brewing Co. workers in Cambridge voted to join United Food and Commercial Workers Local 1445 union.
The unionization affects staff at Lamplighter's Broadway and Cambridge Crossing locations, focusing on wages and workplace safety.
Lamplighter Brewing joins a trend of service industry unionization, including recent efforts in cafes and other food services.

title: Pedestrian hospitalized after being struck by MBTA bus in Norwood
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: A 35-year-old woman was struck by an MBTA bus in Norwood on the 500 block of Washington Street.
The incident occurred at 3:45 p.m. as the woman crossed outside a marked crosswalk, resulting in serious injuries.
She was conscious and in stable condition at the hospital; Transit police are investigating the collision.
